moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad market exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Sell QQQ and SMH as they are flagged 'sell' by the scanner, reducing exposure to underperforming assets.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration and rebalance the portfolio.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero, eliminating high-risk instruments for capital preservation.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure, targeting ~15-20% cash.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on lower drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but potential upside is limited.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and rebalance the broad index exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 10% and sell QQQ and SMH as they are flagged 'sell', exiting underperforming positions.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero, reducing overall portfolio risk.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into a performing tech name within limits.
Buy MSFU and OXY at 10% each, adding high-scoring scanner setups to diversify and add growth.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~10% by reallocating funds from trimmed and sold positions.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad index with quality tech and new high-score setups, aiming for moderate drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, freeing capital for higher growth opportunities.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, SMH, and SPAL to exit underperforming or lower-growth positions.
Increase CRWD to 25% to concentrate in a strong performing tech name.
Add SOXL to 10% to increase leveraged semiconductor exposure, leaning into potential sector growth.
Buy MSFU at 15% as a high-scoring scanner setup, maximizing exposure to strong signals.
Buy SOXS at 5% as a leveraged inverse semiconductor ETF, providing a tactical hedge.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretch growth by using and adding leveraged ETFs and concentrating in high-scoring setups. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reallocate to income-focused assets.
Increase QQQI to 35% to significantly boost monthly distributions and income focus.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, and SMH to exit growth-oriented and high-beta positions.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero, eliminating high-beta and high-risk instruments.
Allocate remaining capital to a cash buffer, targeting a substantial cash position for stability.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth, aligning with the income mandate.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, aiming to park cash for yield and prioritize steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, focusing on low drawdown. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
